steps.stepOne: 1. Fry the mince in a pan with a little oil.

steps.stepTwo: 2. Add the chopped tomatoes & tomato puree. Mix to combine.

steps.stepThree: 3. Cook the mince & tomatoes for roughly 10 - 12 minutes.

steps.stepFour: 4. Place your spaghetti on to cook. Cook until desired toughness.

ingredients.yeast: 8g or one packet Dried Instant Yeast

steps.stepOne: 1. Mix the water, oil and yeast together. Leave for 5 minutes.

steps.stepTwo: 2. In the meantime place your flour and salt into a bowl.

steps.stepThree: 3. Once ready, pour the yeast mix into the flour.

steps.stepFour: 4. Mix with your hands until combined.

steps.stepFive: 5. Tip onto floured work surface and kneed for 15 minutes.

steps.stepSix: 6. Place into lightly oiled bowl. Cover with cling film and leave to rise for an hour.

steps.stepSeven: 7. Once risen, place into parchment lined loaf tin, cover and leave to rise again for 30-40 minutes.

steps.stepEight: 8. Uncover and bake in the oven at 200C for 20-30 minutes or until golden brown.

steps.stepNine: 9. Wait until cool and slice to desired thickness as required.

name: Chicken & Leek Pie
desc: Chicken, leek and delicious gravy encased in pastry.

ingredients.pastry: Shortcrust Pastry (Or puff pastry for the lid if available)

ingredients.gravy: Enough gravy to fill your pie dish

steps.stepOne: 1. Roll out 2/3 of your pastry so it covers the whole of your pie dish.

steps.stepTwo: 2. Combine your leeks and stock in a pan. Bring to the boil and simmer until leeks are soft.

steps.stepThree: 3. In the interim, fry your chicken in a pan with a little oil.

steps.stepFour: 4. Once both are nearly complete, make up your gravy.

steps.stepFive: 5. Place pastry in the pie dish if not done already. Put in your chicken and leeks, then cover with your gravy.

steps.stepSix: 6. Roll out the rest of your pastry to form a lid for the pie dish.

steps.stepSeven: 7. Cover your pie with the lid. Poke several small holes in the lid and bake in the oven at 200C for about 20-25 minutes.

steps.stepEight: 8. Wait to cool slightly before diving and serving.

steps.stepOne: 1. Cream your sugar and butter together.

steps.stepTwo: 2. Mix in the eggs, one at a time, adding about a tablespoon of the flour each time.

steps.stepThree: 3. Add the remaining flour and mix until combined.

steps.stepFour: 4. Grease and line two sandwich tins.

steps.stepFive: 5. Pour half the mixture into each tin. If your tins are too small bake what you have then reuse the tins later.

steps.stepSix: 6. Bake in the oven at 200C for about 10-12 minutes or until a skewer comes out clean when inserted.

steps.stepSeven: 7. Allow to cool. Then layer each cake layer with your choice of filling in between.

name: Cajun Chicken
desc: One pot goodness with a cajun kick.

steps.stepOne: 1. Fry the chicken and onion in a pan with a little oil.

steps.stepTwo: 2. Add the pepper & rice, and fry for another minute or two.

steps.stepThree: 3. Add the seasoning, chopped tomatoes and water. Bring to the boil then cover & simmer for around 20 minutes or until water is absorbed and rice is cooked
